text.title: pnpm Setup and Configuration
text.summary: Toolchain pinning via corepack, .npmrc settings, and initial project setup.

rule.statement: Pin the pnpm version in the root package.json `packageManager` field and activate via `corepack enable`.
rule.rationale: Without pinning, each developer and CI runner uses a different pnpm version, producing lockfile churn and "works on my machine" failures.

example.note: `preinstall` script blocks npm/yarn from being used accidentally.

rule.statement: Keep `shamefully-hoist=false` (the default). Fix phantom dep issues via `.pnpmfile.cjs` `readPackage` hook, never by hoisting.
rule.rationale: `shamefully-hoist=true` defeats pnpm's core benefit of preventing phantom dependencies and makes the project behave like npm.

example.code: pnpm add express zod # Production dep pnpm add -D typescript vitest # Dev dep pnpm add --save-peer react # Peer dep pnpm remove express # Remove pnpm update --latest # Update to latest (ignore semver) pnpm outdated # Show outdated pnpm audit --prod # Audit production deps only pnpm why lodash # Trace why transitive dep is installed
